speaker: alice
text: Starting off green today, Alex, and this one's pretty special - evolutionary biologist Dr Toby Kiers has just won the 2026 Tyler Prize for Environmental Achievement for her work on mycorrhizal fungi. These underground fungi networks are apparently pulling down 13 billion tons of CO2 every year, which is about a third of all fossil fuel emissions.

speaker: charlie
text: That's mental, Jamie. So basically these little fungus networks we can't even see are doing heavy lifting on climate change while we argue about it above ground. Meanwhile, renewable energy just hit 88% of new US electrical capacity in 2025, which is a pretty decent win.

speaker: alice
text: And get this - wind and solar combined overtook fossil fuels as America's leading electricity source in March for the first time ever. Sometimes the good stuff happens while nobody's paying attention, which is exactly how I like my environmental progress - quietly getting on with it.
